page: Microsoft Azure M8-4ms is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the MS server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8280M processor operating at 2.7 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4 vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, alongside 219.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 54.69 GB per core. Additionally, the instance is equipped with 262 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, though it does not include any GPU resources.

Benchmark data reveals significant performance tradeoffs, particularly regarding processing power and memory throughput. In CPU-bound evaluations, the server scored 1035.08 in stress-ng single-core and 2724.76 in multi-core tests, placing it in the bottom 10% and 25% respectively. Memory bandwidth benchmarks also reflect limitations, with read-only large block transfers registering at 17.71 GB/sec and write large block transfers at 17.19 GB/sec. Database operations via Passmark scored 1023.36, while Redis SET operations achieved 2.63 million operations per second, indicating constrained transactional throughput despite the large memory footprint.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the M8-4ms offers high memory density relative to its compute allocation, making it suitable for workloads that require massive memory capacity but have minimal CPU processing demands. The primary tradeoff is the pairing of a large 219.0 GB memory array with weak CPU and memory bandwidth performance. This configuration is appropriate for large in-memory databases, caching layers, or enterprise applications that demand substantial RAM capacity without requiring high-performance parallel computing or intensive mathematical calculations.
description: Microsoft Azure M8-4ms is an x86_64 memory-intensive virtual machine from the MS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is configured with an Intel Xeon 8280M processor running at 2.7 GHz, delivering 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores. The server features 219.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 54.69 GB per core, alongside 262 GB of local storage. Benchmark results indicate a clear performance tradeoff, with CPU and memory bandwidth scores consistently falling into the bottom 10% to 25% categories. For example, stress-ng single-core performance scored 1035.08, and large-block memory read bandwidth reached 17.71 GB/sec. This server is designed for memory-heavy workloads with low compute requirements, such as large-scale caching, in-memory databases, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity without intensive processing needs.

tagline: A memory-intensive virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio for RAM-demanding workloads with minimal compute requirements.

219.0 GB of system memory providing 54.69 GB per core
262 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability

Designed for large in-memory databases and caching layers
